description: Validate loop boundaries, index ranges, and edge-case behavior.
label: RepairPlan
name: BoundaryValidation

description: Ensure loop state changes toward termination on every iteration.
label: RepairPlan
name: LoopProgressRepair

description: Repair loop conditions so execution eventually reaches an exit state.
label: RepairPlan
name: LoopTerminationRepair

description: Guard pointer use and prove dereference validity before access.
label: RepairPlan
name: PointerSafetyRepair

description: Match allocation, ownership transfer, use, and release paths.
label: RepairPlan
name: ResourceLifecycleRepair

description: Clarify ownership and prevent double release or dangling references.
label: RepairPlan
name: OwnershipManagementRepair

description: Ensure all required control-flow paths complete their contract.
label: RepairPlan
name: ControlFlowCompletionRepair

description: Align function behavior with its return type and specification.
label: RepairPlan
name: FunctionContractRepair

description: Define and verify a reachable recursive base case.
label: RepairPlan
name: RecursionTerminationRepair

description: Ensure recursive calls reduce problem size toward the base case.
label: RepairPlan
name: RecursionProgressRepair

description: Avoid use of invalidated iterators, references, and views.
label: RepairPlan
name: IteratorValidityRepair

name: RangeLifetimeRepair

description: Avoid relying on moved-from object values unless reinitialized.
label: RepairPlan
name: MoveSemanticSafetyRepair

description: Move cleanup into exception-safe ownership boundaries.

name: ExceptionSafetyRepair

description: Protect shared mutable state with consistent locking.
label: RepairPlan
name: LockDisciplineRepair

description: Use predicate-based condition variable waiting and notification protocol.
label: RepairPlan
name: ConditionProtocolRepair

description: Prevent unsynchronized cross-thread shared state access.
label: RepairPlan
name: SharedStateProtectionRepair

description: Match formatting placeholders with argument types or use type-safe formatting.

name: FormatTypeSafetyRepair

description: Locate the smallest code region related to the failing behavior.
label: RepairStepTemplate
name: LocateFaultRegion

description: Inspect loop condition and decide whether it should be inclusive or exclusive.
label: RepairStepTemplate
name: InspectLoopCondition

description: Verify that every index stays inside the valid range.
label: RepairStepTemplate
name: VerifyIndexRange

description: Run the smallest edge cases that expose boundary mistakes.
label: RepairStepTemplate
name: RunBoundaryCase

description: Verify that loop state changes toward termination.
label: RepairStepTemplate
name: VerifyLoopProgress

description: Change or add the loop update so progress is guaranteed.
label: RepairStepTemplate
name: RepairLoopUpdate

description: Trace all assignments and ownership transfers of the pointer or handle.
label: RepairStepTemplate
name: TracePointerAssignment

description: Check whether the pointer may be null or invalid before dereference.
label: RepairStepTemplate
name: CheckNullability

description: Add a guard or invariant that prevents invalid dereference.
label: RepairStepTemplate
name: InsertValidityGuard

description: Identify who owns each resource at each program point.
label: RepairStepTemplate
name: VerifyResourceOwner

description: Verify that each allocated resource is released exactly once.
label: RepairStepTemplate
name: VerifyReleasePath

description: Prefer RAII or smart pointers for resource ownership when available.
label: RepairStepTemplate
name: ReplaceManualOwnership

description: Trace whether a resource is accessed after release.
label: RepairStepTemplate
name: TraceReleaseThenUse

description: Stop using handles after release or reset them to a safe state.
label: RepairStepTemplate
name: InvalidateAfterRelease

description: Check every control-flow path in non-void functions for a return value.
label: RepairStepTemplate
name: VerifyAllReturnPaths

description: Add a valid return value or redesign the function contract.
label: RepairStepTemplate
name: AddMissingReturn

description: Identify the recursive base case and when it should apply.
label: RepairStepTemplate
name: IdentifyBaseCase

description: Verify that recursive execution can reach the base case.
label: RepairStepTemplate
name: VerifyBaseCaseReachable

description: Verify that each recursive call reduces the problem size.
label: RepairStepTemplate
name: VerifyRecursiveProgress

description: Rewrite recursive arguments so they progress toward the base case.
label: RepairStepTemplate
name: RewriteRecursiveCall

description: Locate the operation that can invalidate iterators or references.
label: RepairStepTemplate
name: LocateContainerModification

description: Refresh or reacquire iterators after invalidating operations.
label: RepairStepTemplate
name: RefreshIterator

description: Check whether a view or range outlives its source object.
label: RepairStepTemplate
name: CheckViewSourceLifetime

description: Extend source lifetime or use an owning object instead of a dangling view.
label: RepairStepTemplate
name: ExtendSourceLifetime

description: Locate the move operation and later use of the moved-from object.
label: RepairStepTemplate
name: LocateMoveOperation

description: Reinitialize moved-from objects before relying on their semantic value.
label: RepairStepTemplate
name: ReinitializeMovedFromObject

description: Locate deletion through a base pointer or owner.
label: RepairStepTemplate
name: LocatePolymorphicDelete

description: Add a virtual destructor to a polymorphic base class.
label: RepairStepTemplate
name: AddVirtualDestructor

description: Locate paths where exceptions may bypass cleanup or break invariants.
label: RepairStepTemplate
name: IdentifyExceptionPath

description: Move cleanup into RAII-managed objects or scope-bound ownership.
label: RepairStepTemplate
name: ApplyRAIIBoundary

description: Locate the formatting call and its arguments.
label: RepairStepTemplate
name: LocateFormatCall

description: Match placeholders against actual argument types.
label: RepairStepTemplate
name: MatchFormatType

description: Use type-safe formatting APIs where available.
label: RepairStepTemplate
name: UseTypeSafeFormatting

description: Locate mutable state shared across threads.
label: RepairStepTemplate
name: LocateSharedState

description: Check whether all shared-state accesses use the same synchronization discipline.
label: RepairStepTemplate
name: VerifyLockCoverage

description: Use predicate-based wait loops for condition variables.
label: RepairStepTemplate
name: UsePredicateWait

description: Rerun the smallest tests that validate the repair.
label: RepairStepTemplate
name: RerunTargetedTests

description: Replace <= with < when iterating over zero-indexed ranges of length n.
label: RepairAction
name: ReplaceLessEqualWithLess

description: Replace < with <= when the upper bound is meant to be included.
label: RepairAction
name: ReplaceLessWithLessEqual

description: Change loop start value to zero for zero-indexed traversal.
label: RepairAction
name: ChangeLoopStartToZero

description: Add a missing update statement to a loop.
label: RepairAction
name: AddLoopUpdate

description: Change increment to decrement or vice versa when loop progress is reversed.
label: RepairAction
name: ChangeUpdateDirection

description: Insert a condition that prevents out-of-range access.
label: RepairAction
name: InsertBoundsCheck

description: Insert a null check before pointer dereference.
label: RepairAction
name: InsertNullGuard

description: Initialize pointer variables to a safe value.
label: RepairAction
name: InitializePointer

description: Add a missing resource release on the owning path.
label: RepairAction
name: AddMissingFree

description: Remove or restructure duplicated release operations.
label: RepairAction
name: RemoveDuplicateFree

description: Set pointer to null after release when ownership remains in scope.
label: RepairAction
name: SetPointerNullAfterFree

description: Replace raw owning pointer with std::unique_ptr.
label: RepairAction
name: ConvertRawPointerToUniquePtr

description: Add a valid return statement on paths that currently miss one.
label: RepairAction
name: AddReturnStatement

description: Add a recursive base case.
label: RepairAction
name: AddBaseCase

description: Modify recursive arguments to reduce problem size.
label: RepairAction
name: ReduceRecursiveArgument

description: Reacquire iterators after operations that may invalidate them.
label: RepairAction
name: ReacquireIterator

description: Avoid creating non-owning views to temporary or short-lived objects.
label: RepairAction
name: AvoidViewToTemporary

description: Assign a new valid value to a moved-from object before semantic use.
label: RepairAction
name: ReinitializeAfterMove

description: Add virtual destructor to a polymorphic base class.
label: RepairAction
name: AddVirtualDestructor

description: Move manual resource cleanup into RAII objects.
label: RepairAction
name: WrapResourceInRAII

description: Replace incorrect printf-style specifier with the correct one.
label: RepairAction
name: FixPrintfSpecifier

description: Use std::format or std::print for type-safe formatting when available.
label: RepairAction
name: UseStdFormatOrPrint

description: Protect shared-state access with a mutex.
label: RepairAction
name: WrapAccessWithMutex

description: Rewrite condition variable waiting as a predicate-based loop.
label: RepairAction
name: RewriteConditionWait

description: Repair must preserve the problem statement semantics.
label: RepairConstraint
name: PreserveProblemSemantics

description: Prefer the smallest repair that fixes the diagnosed issue.
label: RepairConstraint
name: PreferMinimalPatch

description: Avoid changing unrelated code while repairing the bug.
label: RepairConstraint
name: AvoidUnrelatedRefactor

description: Do not change function signatures unless the function contract is the issue.
label: RepairConstraint
name: KeepPublicAPIStable

description: All index expressions must stay within valid range.
label: RepairConstraint
name: MaintainValidIndexRange

description: Each owned resource must be released exactly once.
label: RepairConstraint
name: ReleaseEachResourceExactlyOnce

description: Do not read or write through a released resource handle.
label: RepairConstraint
name: NoUseAfterRelease

description: Every non-void function path must return a value.
label: RepairConstraint
name: ReturnOnAllRequiredPaths

description: Recursive calls must move toward the base case.
label: RepairConstraint
name: RecursiveCallsMustProgress

description: Do not use invalidated iterators, references, or views.
label: RepairConstraint
name: IteratorMustRemainValid

description: Use moved-from objects only in documented safe ways or reinitialize them.
label: RepairConstraint
name: MovedFromObjectSafeUseOnly

description: Polymorphic base deletion requires a virtual destructor.
label: RepairConstraint
name: PolymorphicBaseRequiresVirtualDestructor

description: Cleanup must happen correctly on normal and exceptional paths.
label: RepairConstraint
name: CleanupMustBeExceptionSafe

description: Format specifiers must match argument types.
label: RepairConstraint
name: FormatSpecifierMustMatchType

description: Shared mutable state must be protected consistently.
label: RepairConstraint
name: SharedStateMustBeSynchronized

description: Condition variable waiting must re-check the predicate under lock.
label: RepairConstraint
name: ConditionWaitNeedsPredicate

afterCode: for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) { sum += a[i]; }
beforeCode: for (int i = 0; i <= n; ++i) { sum += a[i]; }
explanation: When a has n elements, the last valid index is n - 1, so the loop must stop before i reaches n.
label: RepairExample
name: OffByOneLessEqualExample

explanation: Dereference is only safe when the pointer is known to refer to a valid object.
label: RepairExample
name: NullGuardExample

afterCode: int f(bool ok) { if (ok) return 1; return 0; }
explanation: Every path in a non-void function must return a value.
label: RepairExample
name: MissingReturnExample

afterCode: int f(int n) { if (n == 0) return 0; return f(n - 1); }
beforeCode: int f(int n) { if (n == 0) return 0; return f(n); }
explanation: The recursive call must reduce the input toward the base case.
label: RepairExample
name: RecursiveProgressExample

afterCode: struct Base { virtual ~Base() = default; virtual void f(); };
explanation: A polymorphic base class should have a virtual destructor if objects may be deleted through base pointers.
label: RepairExample
name: VirtualDestructorExample

explanation: Condition variable waiting should re-check a predicate under the lock.
label: RepairExample
name: ConditionVariablePredicateWaitExample
